title: Subcommittee #1 (Education) Laird (D-Santa Cruz) Chair, Ochoa Bogh (R-Yucaipa), and Pérez (D-Pasadena) Ensuring Education Funding is Maximized to Help California Students Succeed.
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's funding for education is set to reach nearly $19,000 per pupil for the 2025-26 budget.",
    "This funding represents an increase of nearly 59 percent since the 2019-2020 budget.",
    "California students are below state standards in English language arts and mathematics.",
    "Only 45 percent of high school graduates in California are considered college- or career-ready.",
    "The Delta Tunnels project aims to modernize California's water delivery system.",
    "The Delta Tunnels project is intended to mitigate disruptions caused by sea level rise, levee failures, and earthquakes.",
    "Local water districts have contributed nearly $10 million to the planning and design phases of the Delta Tunnels project.",
    "The current climate bond does not allocate additional funding for the Delta Tunnels project.",
    "Overdoses are the leading cause of young adult mortality in California, with fentanyl often being a factor.",
    "The state's Naloxone Distribution Program provides Narcan, a nasal spray that reverses overdoses, to first responders.",
    "Democrats plan to reroute funds from the Naloxone Distribution Program to harm reduction non-profits.",
    "California provides nearly $1 billion annually to affordable housing programs.",
    "Since 2019, California has provided an additional $12 billion in discretionary one-time initiatives for affordable housing.",
    "State policies, such as prevailing wage and solar requirements, increase the cost of building subsidized housing.",
    "The combination of current policies is believed to work against the state's affordable housing goals.",
    "Nearly 70 percent of voters approved Proposition 36, which mandates treatment for certain offenders.",
    "The Governor's proposed budget does not include funding to implement Proposition 36."
  ]
}
```
